content_of_post_to_answer: Have you ever experienced pressure from other golfers watching you? How do you stay calm and focused in those moments?
answer: The interesting part about pressure from other golfers is that the crowd usually isn't creating the fear; it is revealing where your attention has shifted. The moment you start playing for their reaction instead of the next shot, you've handed them control of your game. Good players don't become unaware of spectators—they simply stop treating observation as judgment. I've written more about this kind of performance mindset on my profile for anyone who wants to explore it further. The hardest opponent on the course is often the version of yourself trying to impress everyone else.
